ads.description: Pinball machine from 1968. Works and plays. Picked this up a few years back for my fiance - has received a lot of play and love, but we're conveniently moving and thought we would give it a post to see if there's interest before we broke it down and packed it up. In a moment of genius, the last time I adjusted the legs, I put one back and front leg on the back AND front, if that makes sense, so it plays on a bit of a right lean. Can be fixed by just swapping the legs around the right way. The P1 score reel also sticks now and then, but comes back to shape after a few extra ticks. You can force it with a little contact switch in the back, but it always kicks up for us after a few seconds. You can see a game starting and a ball reloading in the video. ads.description: A Scatter Dodgeball game set (Swimways/Coop, # 34701). The game set is new and still contained in the original packaging. The packaging does show some (see photos), but it doesn't affect the game components. A great game to add to your collection or to just play and enjoy. About the game... Each player begins with two same-colored wristbands. And the beginning of each round, players form a circle around the dodgeball and one player rolls the dice. Any player who is wearing a wristband that matches the color on the side of the dice when the dice comes to rest may pick up the dodgeball while the other players scatter. If player throwing the dodgeball throws and hits another player, that player has to give the thrower one of their wristbands. If player throws the dodgeball and another player catches it, then player throwing has to give one wristband to the player who caught the ball. First player to collect all the wristbands wins. Game includes: One sting-proof dodgeball. One colored foam dice. Twelve colored wristbands (6 colors). Game rules.

ads.description: Vintage Midway's Space Invaders Parts Catalog" from October 1978, for Game No. 739. All schematics included. About Space Invaders: Space Invaders is a classic fixed shooter arcade game released in 1978 by Taito in Japan and licensed to Midway Manufacturing for distribution in North America. Gameplay: Players control a laser cannon at the bottom of the screen, moving horizontally to shoot down waves of descending aliens and earn points. Cultural Impact: The game was revolutionary for its time, influencing countless other games and shaping popular culture, leading to a shortage of 100-yen coins in Japan due to its immense popularity. Parts Catalog: The catalog shown in the image would contain details and diagrams of the various components used in the Midway Space Invaders arcade machine, assisting with repairs and maintenance.
